title: asplenium ceterach nothosubsp. mantoniae
intro: Asplenium ceterach nothosubsp. mantoniae, also known as the Makarouna fern, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Asplenium ceterach nothosubsp. mantoniae are thought to include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
